content.full_text:
WALLACE A. LAGORIO
Public Relations Counsel                661 Newcastle Road, Newcastle, California, 95658, (916) 663-4322


                                                                                March 19, 1991
Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Proposition 73

Dear Ms Donovan:

     In our on-going work for the Yuba County Water Agency, we publish a quarterly
newsletter which is inserted in the Marysville-Yuba City, Appeal-Democrat for distribution.

     The agency believes that constituents should know who's responsible for setting policy and
so we're planning to publish directors' names as part of the publication's masthead—assuming it is
permitted under the constraints of Prop. 73.

     Even though the measure was passed by voters several years ago, there is still wide-spread
ignorance of its provisions and subsequent interpretations are not fully understood. And so I am
turning to your office for help.

     As I read opinions issued by the Legislative Counsel and the FPPC, it seems clear that we
are permitted to include directors' names within the newsletter's masthead. However, I want to be
sure.

     On page 3 of a June 16, 1988 opinion relative to Prop. 73, Robert E. Leidigh of your
office wrote under "CONCLUSIONS," item 2-g: "... elected officers' names are permitted if
included only in the standard letterhead or logotype of the governmental agency making the
mailing." (A copy of Mr. Leidigh's reply has been attached.)

     I have also attached the proposed masthead copy and would appreciate an opinion as to
whether we can in fact use the names and remain within the law.

     Since we are in the process of going to press for the April edition, a speedy reply would be
greatly appreciated.

                                                                Sincerely,


                                                                WALLACE A. LAGORIO
                                                                Chairman

sections.question: Can we publish directors' names in the masthead of our quarterly newsletter inserted in the Marysville-Yuba City Appeal-Democrat, and remain in compliance with Proposition 73?
sections.question_synthetic: The Yuba County Water Agency seeks clarification on whether including directors' names in a newsletter masthead complies with Proposition 73's restrictions on governmental communications.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The sender references a prior FPPC opinion by Robert E. Leidigh from June 16, 1988, but this current letter is seeking new advice. No conclusion section from FPPC is present in this document. The sender requests urgent response due to April publication deadline.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can we publish directors' names in the masthead of our quarterly newsletter inserted in the Marysville-Yuba City Appeal-Democrat, and remain in compliance with Proposition 73?

embedding.summary: Wallace Laconto, public relations counsel for the Yuba County Water Agency, requests FPPC guidance on whether the agency may include directors' names in the masthead of its quarterly newsletter distributed through the Appeal-Democrat newspaper, in compliance with Proposition 73.
